original_hypothesis.title: Emissions Permanently Lost When GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount Called With Zero totalWeight
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When MinterUpgradeable.update_period() calls GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount(_gauge), BLACK tokens are transferred to GaugeManager. If IVoter(voter).totalWeight() returns 0 (no active votes), _ratio = 0, and the global index is not updated. The transferred tokens remain in GaugeManager with no mechanism to recover or redistribute them. They are permanently stuck.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The tokens are transferred via safeTransferFrom before the totalWeight check. When _ratio = 0, index remains unchanged, meaning no gauge can ever claim these tokens through the normal distribution mechanism. There is no sweep or recovery function in GaugeManager for the base token.

proof_of_signal.pattern: safeTransferFrom executes before totalWeight check; if totalWeight=0, _ratio=0, index unchanged, tokens stuck

preconditions: All veNFT holders reset votes before epoch flip
Or: no votes have been cast (e.g., early protocol stages)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: if(totalWeight > 0) _ratio = amount * 1e18 / totalWeight; else _ratio = 0 and tokens are stuck
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Coordinate vote resets before epoch flip, or exploit AVM reset timing
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Entire epoch emission permanently lost - reduces circulating supply but destroys intended incentives

economic_questions: Has totalWeight ever been 0 at epoch boundary?
Can AVM reset timing create a window where totalWeight = 0?
What is the value of one epoch's emissions?
validation_requirements: Check if Voter ensures minimum totalWeight or if zero is possible
Analyze AVM reset/vote cycle timing relative to epoch flip
Confirm no recovery mechanism in GaugeManager

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Large veNFT position to control voting weight (griefing attack)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: No direct profit - griefing that destroys one epoch of emissions
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Negative for attacker (gas costs), destructive for protocol

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Sufficient veNFT to represent significant weight
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Just before epoch flip when AVM hasn't re-voted yet
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of one epoch's gauge emissions

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Full weekly emission amount (10M BLACK initially)

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: weekly emission amount, current totalWeight dynamics
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify no minimum totalWeight enforcement in Voter

validation_result.mechanism: unrecoverable token lock on zero totalWeight
validation_result.impact_summary: When GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount is called with amount > 0 and IVoter(voter).totalWeight() == 0, BLACK tokens are transferred into GaugeManager but the global index is not updated, permanently stranding those tokens with no recovery mechanism.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The defect is in GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount(). The function unconditionally transfers tokens from the minter via safeTransferFrom BEFORE checking totalWeight. When totalWeight == 0, _ratio is 0, index is unchanged, and the transferred tokens are never attributed to any gauge's claimable balance. Future calls to notifyRewardAmount with non-zero totalWeight will only account for the NEW amount transferred in that call - the previously stuck tokens remain as untracked excess balance in GaugeManager. There is no sweep, recovery, or emergency withdraw function in GaugeManager for the base token. The only outflows of base tokens from GaugeManager are: (1) gauge distribution via _distribute → claimable accounting, and (2) killGauge returning claimable to minter. Neither mechanism can reclaim tokens that were never indexed.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof of signal is accurate. GaugeManager.sol notifyRewardAmount (approx lines 213-225) shows the safeTransferFrom executing before the totalWeight check, and the conditional index update that does nothing when totalWeight is 0. MinterUpgradeable.sol update_period() calls notifyRewardAmount unconditionally with no check for zero totalWeight.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Tokens are transferred unconditionally via safeTransferFrom before the totalWeight check. When totalWeight == 0, _ratio = 0 and index is not updated. The tokens sit in GaugeManager with no accounting reference and no mechanism to recover them. They are permanently stuck.
The minter calls notifyRewardAmount with _gauge amount without any check on whether totalWeight is zero. If there are no votes, _gauge worth of tokens will be permanently locked in GaugeManager.

calling_sequence: 5. GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount() executes safeTransferFrom(minter, this, amount) - tokens now in GaugeManager
8. No recovery path exists in GaugeManager for these tokens
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: MinterUpgradeable.update_period() -> _gaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount(_gauge) -> IERC20Upgradeable(base).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount) transfers tokens in, then IVoter(voter).totalWeight() returns 0, so _ratio = 0, index += 0 (no-op). Tokens are now in GaugeManager balance but not tracked by any accounting (not in any gauge's claimable, not in the index). GaugeManager has no emergencyRecoverERC20 or sweep function - only outflows are claimable-based distributions and killGauge returns to minter.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that configure minimum vote weights or guard against zero totalWeight scenarios.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation:
Scenario 1 (early protocol): No votes have been cast yet. update_period is called (either manually or via EpochController.performUpkeep). _gauge worth of BLACK tokens are minted and transferred to GaugeManager. totalWeight is 0, so they are permanently stuck.

Scenario 2 (griefing): All veNFT holders (or a dominant holder controlling most voting weight) reset their votes just before the epoch flip. When update_period is triggered, totalWeight is 0, and the entire epoch's gauge emissions are permanently locked in GaugeManager.

Scenario 3 (AVM timing): If the AVM (Auto Voting Manager) resets votes before re-voting, there could be a window where totalWeight is 0 at the exact time update_period is called.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is HIGH. An entire epoch's gauge emissions (initial weekly of 10M BLACK minus team and rebase portions) can be permanently lost. In the early protocol stages this is especially likely as no votes may exist. The lost tokens reduce intended LP incentives and cannot be recovered through any on-chain mechanism. At worst, this represents a recurring loss each epoch if the condition persists.
recommended_mitigations: Add a check in notifyRewardAmount to revert or skip transfer when totalWeight == 0: require(IVoter(voter).totalWeight() > 0, 'no votes');
Alternatively, accumulate unallocated amounts: store the undistributed amount and add it to the next epoch's distribution when totalWeight > 0
Add an emergency recovery function for the base token restricted to GAUGE_ADMIN or team multisig
In update_period, check totalWeight before calling notifyRewardAmount and retain tokens in the minter if totalWeight is 0
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy GaugeManager, MinterUpgradeable, Black, a mock Voter with totalWeight() returning 0, and a mock VotingEscrow. 2. Initialize all contracts and link them. 3. Complete _initialize on Minter to enable update_period. 4. Advance time past one WEEK. 5. Call update_period(). 6. Observe that BLACK tokens are transferred to GaugeManager but index remains 0. 7. Verify no mechanism can extract these tokens from GaugeManager. 8. Now set Voter.totalWeight() > 0, advance another week, call update_period again. 9. Observe that only the new epoch's tokens are distributed - the first epoch's tokens remain stuck.
uncertainties: The Voter contract source is not in scope - we cannot verify whether totalWeight() can truly return 0 in practice, though the interface returns uint256 with no minimum guarantee
Whether the AVM reset timing could create a zero-weight window depends on the AVM implementation which is also out of scope
The practical likelihood during normal operation (after initial phase) depends on vote participation patterns
additional_testing_needed: Verify Voter contract's totalWeight behavior at epoch boundaries
Test with actual AVM to determine if vote reset/re-vote creates zero-weight windows
Quantify the probability of zero totalWeight during normal protocol operation
Verify that GaugeManager has no storage variable or proxy-level mechanism that could be used to extract stuck tokens
